title: OpenAI’s Head of Safety Is Leaving the Company
gist: OpenAI's head of safety systems, Johannes Heidecke, is leaving the company following a reorganization that merged OpenAI's safety and research teams.

insights.claim: Johannes Heidecke, OpenAI's head of safety systems, told staff this week that he is leaving the company.
insights.speaker: Maxwell Zeff
insights.so_what: A safety-leadership exit at OpenAI is a signal worth tracking for governance and regulatory risk as the company scales commercial and hardware ambitions.
insights.quote: OpenAI’s head of safety systems, Johannes Heidecke, told staff this week that he’s leaving the company, WIRED has learned.

insights.claim: Heidecke's departure follows an internal reorganization at OpenAI that sought to integrate its safety and research teams.
insights.speaker: Maxwell Zeff
insights.so_what: Merging safety into research functions could signal deprioritization of independent safety oversight, a governance risk investors and regulators may want to monitor.
insights.quote: Heidecke’s departure follows a reorganization that sought to integrate OpenAI’s safety and research teams.
